name: Zariski–Lipman Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The algebraic variety X over a field k, with its structure sheaf and the universal property of the tangent sheaf Ω¹_X/k

VINIT.justification: Before any geometric or algebraic structure is imposed, the bare set X exists in void state — no sheaf, no derivations, no smoothness criterion.

TANCH.chosen_element: The variety X with its structure sheaf O_X
TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains and closes the entire system: X is the ambient space in which all derivations, tangent vectors, and smoothness are defined.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the universal derivation that maps regular functions to differential 1-forms, a directed transformation toward the tangent sheaf.

AREV.chosen_element: Contraction map ι: Ω¹_X/k → O_X (via pairing with vector fields)
AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: the dual operation that contracts differential forms back to functions, descent from the cotangent to the base ring.

CLINK.chosen_element: Composition of derivations: D₁ ∘ D₂ for D₁, D₂ ∈ Der_k(O_X)
CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of derivations; the Lie bracket [D₁, D₂] = D₁ ∘ D₂ − D₂ ∘ D₁ is the composition structure on the derivation module.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the identity element in the derivation algebra; the element that recognizes itself as the trivial derivation.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Smoothness criterion split: X splits into {smooth locus X_sm, singular locus X_sing}
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: the Zariski–Lipman theorem branches the variety into two disjoint open/closed subsets based on whether the tangent sheaf Ω¹_X/k is locally free (smooth) or not.

FFUSE.chosen_element: Reconstruction of X from smooth and singular loci
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the union X_sm ∪ X_sing = X recovers the original variety exactly. FFUSE(FSPLIT(X)) = X holds: the disjoint union of the two loci reconstitutes X.

EVALT.chosen_element: Smooth locus X_sm: Ω¹_X/k is locally free (projective module)
EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: at smooth points, the tangent sheaf is a vector bundle, the Zariski–Lipman condition is satisfied, and the variety is regular.

EVALF.chosen_element: Singular locus X_sing: Ω¹_X/k is not locally free
EVALF.justification: Negative branch: at singular points, the tangent sheaf fails to be locally free, the Zariski–Lipman condition is violated, and the variety is singular.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: Boundary points where smoothness is undecidable or transitional
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: at certain special points (e.g., nodes, cusps in characteristic p), the tangent sheaf may exhibit both locally-free and non-locally-free behavior simultaneously, held without resolution.

IFIX.chosen_element: Permanent record: the classification of X as smooth or singular, fixed in the Zariski topology
IFIX.justification: Irreversible fixation: once the smoothness locus is determined by the Zariski–Lipman theorem, this classification is append-only and cannot be undone within the given field and variety.

phase_2.split_input: Variety X with tangent sheaf Ω¹_X/k
split_outputs: Singular locus X_sing (Ω¹_X/k not locally free)
phase_2.fuse_result: X = X_sm ∪ X_sing (disjoint union in Zariski topology)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — Uninitialized variety: no structure sheaf, no derivations, no tangent sheaf defined.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — Smooth variety: Ω¹_X/k is locally free (projective), all points satisfy the Zariski–Lipman condition, X is regular.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — Singular variety: Ω¹_X/k is not locally free at some point, the Zariski–Lipman condition fails, X has singular locus.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Paradice (mixed smoothness): the variety exhibits both smooth and singular behavior simultaneously, or the tangent sheaf is locally free in some étale neighborhood but not globally, held without resolution.

steps.domain_action: Uninitialized variety X — void state before any algebraic structure.
Impose structure sheaf O_X and close the system within the variety X.
Define universal derivation d: O_X → Ω¹_X/k — forward morphism to tangent sheaf.
Compose derivations via Lie bracket [D₁, D₂] — sequential chaining of tangent vector operations.
Identity derivation id ∈ Der_k(O_X) — self-reference in the derivation algebra.
Branch X into smooth locus X_sm and singular locus X_sing based on local freeness of Ω¹_X/k.
Affirm smooth locus X_sm — Ω¹_X/k is locally free (projective module), Zariski–Lipman holds.
Forward morphism on smooth locus — tangent vectors form a vector bundle.
Record smooth points as permanent classification in Zariski topology.
Negate singular locus X_sing — Ω¹_X/k fails to be locally free.
Reverse morphism on singular locus — contraction of non-free cotangent forms.
Record singular points as permanent classification in Zariski topology.
Identify boundary/transitional points where smoothness is paradoxical (both locally free and not, held simultaneously).
Reconstitute X = X_sm ∪ X_sing — disjoint union recovers original variety exactly.
Compose the full classification cycle — derivation module structure determines smoothness, smoothness determines locus partition, partition determines variety structure.
Self-referential closure — the variety X recognizes itself through its tangent sheaf Ω¹_X/k and the Zariski–Lipman criterion.
Terminal anchor — the Zariski–Lipman theorem is complete; no further refinement within this algebraic structure.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Algebraic geometry interpreter: translates the domain intention 'determine smoothness of X' into the computation of Ω¹_X/k and the local-freeness test at each point.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Sheaf cohomology and étale topology: components (smooth locus, singular locus, derivation module) communicate via sheaf sections and localization maps.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Structure sheaf O_X and its modules: state is stored as the tangent sheaf Ω¹_X/k and the classification of points into smooth/singular loci.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Zariski topology order: operations proceed from generic points (smooth) to special points (singular), respecting the specialization order on X.
phase_5.alfs_store: The universal property of Ω¹_X/k: the canonical reference store is the module of Kähler differentials, which bootstraps all derivations and smoothness criteria.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Computation of local freeness at each point: rank of Ω¹_X/k modulo the maximal ideal, repeated for all points in X.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Variety X with structure sheaf O_X, tangent sheaf Ω¹_X/k unclassified.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Variety X partitioned into smooth locus X_sm (Ω¹_X/k locally free) and singular locus X_sing (Ω¹_X/k not locally free), classification fixed.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The Zariski–Lipman cycle is entropy-conservative: the information content of X is preserved through the FSPLIT/FFUSE pair; the disjoint union X_sm ∪ X_sing contains exactly the same geometric data as X, merely reorganized by smoothness criterion. No information is created or destroyed, only partitioned and re-fused.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → IFIX → EVALF → AREV → IFIX → ENGAGR → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Zariski–Lipman Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(10,2,3,2)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=17
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 13)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [8] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [9] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [14] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [15] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [16] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def zariski_lipman_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Uninitialized variety X — void state before any algebraic structure.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Impose structure sheaf O_X and close the system within the variety X.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Define universal derivation d: O_X → Ω¹_X/k — forward morphism to tangent sheaf.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose derivations via Lie bracket [D₁, D₂] — sequential chaining of tangent...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Identity derivation id ∈ Der_k(O_X) — self-reference in the derivation algebra.)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch X into smooth locus X_sm and singular locus X_sing based on local freeness of Ω¹_X/k.) / FFUSE [13] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Affirm smooth locus X_sm — Ω¹_X/k is locally free (projective module), Zarisk...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism on smooth locus — tangent vectors form a vector bundle.)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record smooth points as permanent classification in Zariski topology.)
      -- F-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Negate singular locus X_sing — Ω¹_X/k fails to be locally free.)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse morphism on singular locus — contraction of non-free cotangent forms.)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record singular points as permanent classification in Zariski topology.)
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Identify boundary/transitional points where smoothness is paradoxical (both l...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [13]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [13] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute X = X_sm ∪ X_sing — disjoint union recovers original variety exactly.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [14] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the full classification cycle — derivation module structure determine...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [15] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-referential closure — the variety X recognizes itself through its tangen...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [16] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Terminal anchor — the Zariski–Lipman theorem is complete; no further refineme...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def zariski_lipman_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (zariski_lipman_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def zariski_lipman_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (zariski_lipman_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem zariski_lipman_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
